reason: AGI has not been declared achieved by frontier labs as of 2026-03-02 (supports skepticism), but capabilities advanced substantially with GPT-5, so broad anti-progress framing is not fully supported.

GPT-5 was released (not deferred indefinitely) and OpenAI valuation remained high, which conflicts with strong near-term deflation scenarios; broader governance skepticism remains partly interpretive.
Guardrail/common-sense concerns persist, while formal regulation has advanced (e.g., EU AI Act timetable), yielding mixed directionality.
Frontier model documentation still reports non-zero hallucination/error rates and explicitly notes ongoing hallucination limitations.
Theme appears weakly aligned to copyright/IP and representative claims are broad methodological questions without clear measurable endpoints.
Claim concerns long-run valuation viability of pure LLM plays; as of 2026-03-02 evidence is inconclusive and horizon remains open.
Contains AGI-by-2029 style timeline references; deadline has not passed as of 2026-03-02.
Broad skepticism about deep learning doing 'everything' remains open-ended without concrete endpoint/metric.
Normative/common-sense architecture prescription; valuable but not directly falsifiable as stated.
Normative claim about what is required for AGI (abstraction/compositionality) lacks agreed operational test and deadline.
